issues.summary: PosGroup constants live in the query package but are imported by config and tagging layers, reversing the expected dependency direction
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.PosGroup and calls PosGroup.ADJ etc. in collocatePosGroup()
SimpleTagger imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.PosGroup and uses it in TaggedToken.getPosGroup()
PosGroup is a domain-level constant (4 string values) that does not belong to the query layer
issues.suggestion: Move PosGroup to a shared domain or constants package (e.g., pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.domain or .common) so that config, tagging, and query all depend on it without any package depending on query for a constant.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer's primary constructor directly instantiates BlackLabQueryExecutor, bypassing the QueryExecutor abstraction it also accepts
evidence: Line 71: public SemanticFieldExplorer(String indexPath) throws IOException { this.executor = new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath); }
Line 76: A second constructor takes QueryExecutor (for testing), showing the abstraction exists but the production path bypasses it
The ownsExecutor flag is a code smell indicating lifecycle management is being manually layered on top of an inconsistent creation pattern
issues.suggestion: Remove the String-path constructor or make it a static factory; let callers always inject a QueryExecutor. If lifecycle ownership is needed, provide an explicit factory method SemanticFieldExplorer.forIndex(String) that documents the ownership contract.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer imports five inner result classes from SemanticFieldExplorer, tightly coupling the API layer to exploration implementation internals
evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective
All five are accessed by field name (e.g., dn.noun, dn.sharedCount, ca.adjective) in the API handlers, encoding the data model in two layers simultaneously
Any rename or restructuring of SemanticFieldExplorer's result classes requires changes in the API layer
issues.suggestion: Expose SemanticFieldExplorer results through stable accessor methods or move the result types to a shared results package so the API layer does not depend on inner-class details of SemanticFieldExplorer.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer is a 1000+ line monolith that handles routing, parameter parsing, validation, business orchestration, and JSON serialization for 10+ endpoints
evidence: start() method is 230+ lines registering all endpoints inline with full lambda bodies
Handler methods such as handleSemanticFieldExplore (130+ lines) combine parameter parsing, validation, executor calls, and full JSON response construction
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti duplicates the parameter parsing, seed validation, and edge-building pattern from handleSemanticFieldExplore
issues.suggestion: Break the server into focused handler classes (one per endpoint group) that receive pre-wired dependencies. Extract a separate ResponseBuilder or serialize the explorer result classes directly. The start() method should only contain route registration.

issues.summary: The query package subsumes executor infrastructure, result DTOs, snippet parsing utilities, semantic field exploration logic, and POS group constants — too many concerns for a single package
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor: query execution infrastructure
BlackLabSnippetParser: XML/text parsing utility
SemanticFieldExplorer: multi-step exploration domain logic (700+ lines with its own inner result class hierarchy)
QueryResults: shared result DTOs
PosGroup: domain constants
issues.suggestion: Split the query package: keep query execution infrastructure in query/, move SemanticFieldExplorer + its result types to exploration/ or semantic/, move BlackLabSnippetParser to query/internal or parsing/, and move PosGroup to a shared constants package.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer serves as both a service (exploration/comparison logic) and a result data container (6 static inner result classes), blurring its responsibility
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer contains: ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, ComparisonResult, AdjectiveProfile, Edge — all static inner classes
The service methods exploreByPattern() and compare() are in the same file as 6 result/DTO types totalling ~300 lines of inner class definitions
API layer must import individual inner classes: import SemanticFieldExplorer.ComparisonResult, etc.
issues.suggestion: Extract the result types (ExplorationResult, ComparisonResult, etc.) to their own top-level classes or a dedicated results sub-package. SemanticFieldExplorer should only contain the service logic.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor declares executeBcqlQuery and executeSurfacePattern as default methods that fall back to lower-level calls, but there is only one real implementation, making the defaults unused scaffolding
evidence: QueryExecutor.executeBcqlQuery() default: return executeQuery(bcqlPattern, maxResults) — comment says 'for backward compatibility'
QueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern() default: return findCollocations(lemma, bcqlPattern, ...) — comment says 'ignores position hints'
BlackLabQueryExecutor overrides both, so the defaults are never exercised in the production code path
getExecutorType() default returns getClass().getSimpleName() — useful pattern, but the other two defaults only exist to ease a non-existent alternate implementation
issues.suggestion: If only one implementation exists, declare executeBcqlQuery and executeSurfacePattern as abstract interface methods (no defaults) to make the contract explicit. Remove the backward-compatibility comment which implies an obsolete migration that is already complete.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser exposes seven near-identical methods for extracting lemmas from XML or text at a given position, creating a fragmented API with overlapping responsibilities
evidence: extractLemmaFromMatch(String matchText) — finds last lemma in XML
extractCollocateFromSnippet(String snippet) — also finds last lemma in XML
extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition(String xmlSnippet, int position) — finds lemma at 1-based position in XML
extractCollocateFromMatch(String matchOnly, int labelPos) — same as above, different name
extractTokenFromSnippet(String snippet, int position) — same pattern, falls back to last lemma
extractCollocateFromMatchText(String matchText, int position) — whitespace-separated variant
extractCollocateFromPlainText(String text, int position) — another whitespace-separated variant
extractLemmaFromMatch and extractCollocateFromSnippet do exactly the same thing (iterate LEMMA_ATTR, return last)
issues.suggestion: Consolidate to two clear methods: extractLemmaAtPosition(String xml, int position) for XML and extractTokenAtPosition(String text, int position) for plain text. Remove duplicates and document the contract (0 = last, n = nth).

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer instantiates SemanticFieldExplorer in four separate handler methods, creating a new wrapper object per request that adds no state beyond the already-held executor reference
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor); ... explorer.close();
handleSemanticField: same pattern
handleSemanticFieldExamples: same pattern
SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor) constructor stores only the executor reference — no per-instance state is added beyond what the server already holds
issues.suggestion: Hold a single SemanticFieldExplorer as a field of WordSketchApiServer (initialized in the constructor), or make the exploration methods static taking a QueryExecutor parameter. Either eliminates the per-request create/close cycle.

issues.summary: pom.xml declares com.alibaba:fastjson at version 2.0.25, but all code imports from com.alibaba.fastjson2.* — the correct artifact for that package is com.alibaba:fastjson2
evidence: pom.xml line 45: <artifactId>fastjson</artifactId> <version>2.0.25</version>
HttpApiUtils.java line 3: import com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSON;
WordSketchApiServer.java line 1-3: import com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSON; import com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSONArray; import com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSONObject;
The com.alibaba.fastjson2.* namespace belongs to artifact com.alibaba:fastjson2, not com.alibaba:fastjson
issues.suggestion: Change pom.xml to declare <artifactId>fastjson2</artifactId> with the same version to make the dependency explicit. The current setup works only if fastjson2 classes are present via a transitive dependency from BlackLab or another library, which is fragile.

issues.summary: pom.xml defines a lucene.version property (8.11.1) that is never used as a direct dependency, creating a false impression of the Lucene version in use
evidence: pom.xml line 18: <lucene.version>8.11.1</lucene.version>
No dependency in the pom.xml references ${lucene.version}
Lucene is only brought in transitively through blacklab-engine; BlackLab 4.0.0 bundles a different Lucene version than 8.11.1
issues.suggestion: Remove the unused lucene.version property. If it was intended as documentation, add a comment explaining which Lucene version BlackLab 4.0 pulls in transitively.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer.start() is a 230+ line method registering 10 endpoint handlers inline with full request handling logic in each lambda body
evidence: start() registers /health, /api/sketch/, /api/relations, /api/relations/dep, /api/semantic-field/explore, /api/semantic-field/explore-multi, /api/semantic-field, /api/semantic-field/examples, /api/concordance/examples, /api/visual/radial, /api/bcql — all inline
Each handler lambda calls a private handleXxx method, but OPTIONS/method guard boilerplate is duplicated in every lambda body rather than being extracted
issues.suggestion: Extract the repeated OPTIONS/method-guard pattern into HttpApiUtils or a helper. Optionally use a dispatcher map or register individual endpoint methods directly to reduce start() to route registration only.

issues.summary: The pattern of building a JSONObject per collocate (lemma, frequency, logDice, pos) and appending to a JSONArray is duplicated verbatim across four handler methods
evidence: handleRelationQuery lines 361-369: for loop building JSONObject word with lemma/frequency/logDice/pos
handleDependencyRelationQuery lines 462-470: identical loop
handleFullSketch lines 318-327: identical loop inside an outer relation loop
handleFullDependencySketch lines 414-423: identical loop inside an outer relation loop
issues.suggestion: Extract a private static JSONObject toCollocateJson(WordSketchResult r) helper and call it from all four sites. This reduces 40+ duplicated lines to 4 calls.

issues.summary: SimpleTagger's constructor contains ~80 hard-coded lexicon.put() calls for word-to-tag mappings, embedding test corpus data as production code
evidence: SimpleTagger() constructor lines 113-210 contain explicit put() calls for words like house, dog, cat, park, bed, hill, man, garden, etc.
Comments like '// More adjectives from test corpus' and '// Common nouns' confirm this is test data baked into production code
The loadLexicon(String file) method already exists to load from a file, but the constructor pre-populates with hard-coded entries instead of delegating to it
issues.suggestion: Move the hard-coded entries to a classpath resource file (e.g., /tagger/default_lexicon.txt). Load it in the constructor with loadLexicon(). This keeps the code clean and makes the lexicon maintainable without recompilation.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer implements AutoCloseable but is created and closed manually in handler methods, so exceptions between creation and close() leak resources
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor); ... explorer.close(); — no try-with-resources
handleSemanticField: same pattern
handleSemanticFieldExamples: same pattern
If the business logic between creation and close() throws, close() is never called
issues.suggestion: Wrap all three instantiations in try (SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor)) { ... } to guarantee close() is always called. Alternatively, as noted elsewhere, make the explorer a shared field.

issues.summary: RadialPlot.toSVG() mixes string concatenation with StringBuilder.append and String.format in an inconsistent style within the same method
evidence: Line 163: svg.append("<svg ... width=\"" + width + "\" height=\"" + height + ..." — string concatenation inside append
Line 177: svg.append("  <rect width=\"" + width + "\" height=\"" + height + ...) — same pattern
Line 168: svg.append(String.format("    .guide-circle { ...stroke-width: %s; }\n", fmt(...))) — uses String.format
Line 163 and 177 could use String.format or a template for consistency with adjacent lines
issues.suggestion: Pick one string-building style for the SVG template lines. Using String.format consistently (already used for most lines) is clearest. Alternatively, use a simple template with placeholders and replace all at once.

issues.summary: JSON response assembly is repeated verbatim across five or more handler methods with no shared assembler
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticField, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleFullSketch, and handleFullDependencySketch each build their response Map<String,Object> by hand
Math.round(x * 100.0) / 100.0 rounding idiom appears at least six times across these methods
collocations JSONArray construction (word.put lemma/frequency/logDice/pos) is copy-pasted into handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleDependencyRelationQuery, and handleFullDependencySketch
issues.suggestion: Introduce a thin response-mapper layer (e.g. ApiMapper or ResultSerializer) that converts QueryResults.WordSketchResult and SemanticFieldExplorer result types to their JSON representations in one place; all handlers call the mapper

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and extractPosFromGroup duplicate BlackLabSnippetParser logic by directly accessing its static Pattern fields
evidence: extractDependentLemmaFromGroup opens a Matcher on BlackLabSnippetParser.LEMMA_ATTR and iterates to find the last lemma — identical to BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaFromMatch
extractPosFromGroup opens a Matcher on BlackLabSnippetParser.XPOS_ATTR then falls back to UPOS_ATTR — identical to BlackLabSnippetParser.extractPosFromMatch
BlackLabSnippetParser already exposes extractLemmaFromMatch(String) and extractPosFromMatch(String) as package-private statics for exactly this purpose
issues.suggestion: Delete extractDependentLemmaFromGroup and extractPosFromGroup; replace their call sites with BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaFromMatch(identity) and BlackLabSnippetParser.extractPosFromMatch(identity)

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer is constructed, used, and closed inline in three separate handler methods instead of being shared or managed by a factory
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor); ... explorer.close();
handleSemanticField: same pattern repeated
handleSemanticFieldExamples: same pattern repeated a third time
SemanticFieldExplorer holds no persistent state between calls, making it a stateless collaborator whose lifecycle management adds noise to the handlers
issues.suggestion: Either make SemanticFieldExplorer stateless (no Closeable) or inject it as a field of WordSketchApiServer alongside executor, removing the repeated create/close boilerplate from every handler

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExplore calls rel.getFullPattern(seed) to substitute the headword, but handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti uses the raw rel.pattern() without substitution
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore: String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().getFullPattern(seed);
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().pattern(); (no head substitution)
Both handlers pass bcqlPattern to executor.executeSurfacePattern, meaning explore-multi queries execute with an unsubstituted template pattern
issues.suggestion: In handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, derive the per-seed bcql pattern inside the seed loop via rel.getFullPattern(seed) rather than using the raw pattern once before the loop

issues.summary: handleConcordanceExamples manually replaces HTML entity & after URL decoding, a concern that belongs in the parsing layer
evidence: if (word1 != null) word1 = word1.replace("&", "&");
if (word2 != null) word2 = word2.replace("&", "&");
HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams already handles URLDecoder.decode; HTML entity decoding is a separate concern that should either be handled there or not at all
issues.suggestion: Move HTML entity unescaping into HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams or a shared normalization helper so handlers receive clean values without additional glue code

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser carries Javadoc comments on most methods that merely restate the method signature without adding insight
evidence: /** Extract lemma from matched text (XML format). */ static String extractLemmaFromMatch — the comment says exactly what the name says
/** Extract POS tag from matched text (XML format). */ static String extractPosFromMatch — same issue
/** Extract plain text from XML snippet by stripping tags. */ static String extractPlainTextFromXml — again fully restated by the name
/** Trim left/match/right plain-text parts to a single sentence. */ on trimToSentence — the one genuinely useful comment on this file, but surrounded by trivial ones
issues.suggestion: Remove or collapse the trivial Javadoc stubs; keep only comments that explain the WHY (e.g. why fallback to upos, why last lemma heuristic)

issues.summary: extractXposPattern and extractPosPattern in GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig are single-line pass-through wrappers with no added logic
evidence: private static String extractXposPattern(String constraint) { return extractConstraintAttribute(constraint, "xpos"); }
private static String extractPosPattern(String constraint) { return extractConstraintAttribute(constraint, "tag"); }
Both are only called once each from mergeLemmaConstraint, making them name-only indirections
issues.suggestion: Inline both calls into mergeLemmaConstraint: extractConstraintAttribute(existingConstraint, "xpos") and extractConstraintAttribute(existingConstraint, "tag") are self-documenting at the call site

issues.summary: LogDiceCalculator.compute(long, long, long) is a pure cast-and-delegate with a restating Javadoc
evidence: public static double compute(long collocateFreq, long headwordFreq, long collocateTotal) { return compute((double) collocateFreq, (double) headwordFreq, (double) collocateTotal); }
/** Compute logDice score from frequency counts. */ — the comment adds nothing beyond the method name and parameter types
issues.suggestion: The overload itself is useful convenience; just remove the trivial Javadoc stub so the signal-to-noise ratio improves

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti logs its own entry and every input parameter at INFO level before doing any work
evidence: logger.info("handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti called");
logger.info("Query string: {}", query);
logger.info("Seeds parameter: {}", seedsStr);
No other handler in the same class uses this entry/parameter logging pattern at INFO — the inconsistency further suggests it was added ad hoc
issues.suggestion: Remove the three INFO-level entry logs; the existing request URI log on line 199 is sufficient; parameter-level tracing belongs at DEBUG

issues.summary: boolean raw is declared and populated but never used in handleBcqlQueryPost
evidence: boolean raw = obj.getBooleanValue("raw");  // Add raw output option
raw is never referenced after this assignment; the comment 'Add raw output option' suggests an incomplete implementation that was left in
issues.suggestion: Either implement the raw output path or remove the variable and comment entirely

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader silently falls back to the old 'cql_pattern' JSON field when 'pattern' is absent, but the class Javadoc still documents the old field name as canonical
evidence: String pattern = relObj.containsKey("pattern") ? relObj.getString("pattern") : (relObj.containsKey("cql_pattern") ? relObj.getString("cql_pattern") : "");
The class-level Javadoc example shows '"cql_pattern": "[tag=jj.*]"' — the old field name — as the expected structure
No deprecation warning is emitted when the fallback path is taken, making it invisible at runtime
issues.suggestion: Either remove the cql_pattern fallback if no live configs use it, or emit a deprecation warning via logger.warn when it is used, and update the Javadoc to show 'pattern' as the canonical field

issues.summary: QueryExecutor default methods exist solely for backward compatibility with implementations that no longer exist
evidence: executeBcqlQuery default: '// Default: treat BCQL as CQL (for backward compatibility)'
executeSurfacePattern default: '// Default: fall back to findCollocations (ignores position hints)'
QueryExecutor Javadoc: 'Allows different implementations (legacy, hybrid, BlackLab) to be used interchangeably' — but only BlackLabQueryExecutor exists in the codebase
getExecutorType return example says '"legacy", "hybrid"' — neither type is implemented
issues.suggestion: Remove the default implementations and the mention of legacy/hybrid; if the interface is meant to allow future alternative implementations, document that intent rather than preserving dead compatibility code

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.posGroupFromConstraint handles both 'xpos=' (current) and 'tag=' (legacy) attribute names with no migration path or deprecation signal
evidence: Comment: '// xpos (Penn Treebank — used by current grammar)' followed by 'tag (legacy / alternative attribute name)'
Twelve tag= branches mirror twelve xpos= branches without any log warning when the legacy path triggers
No test or comment indicates whether any active grammar config file still uses tag= attributes
issues.suggestion: Audit active grammar JSON files; if tag= is unused, remove the legacy branches and their comment; if still in use, add a logger.warn deprecation notice

issues.summary: The query/ package conflates four distinct concerns: query execution, XML parsing, data-transfer objects, and semantic exploration
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor: query execution against a BlackLab index
BlackLabSnippetParser: stateless XML/text transformation utility for parsing BlackLab output
QueryResults: plain DTO classes (WordSketchResult, ConcordanceResult) with no query logic
SemanticFieldExplorer: a higher-level exploration algorithm that composes query results
PosGroup: a constants file imported by config/ and api/ packages, not a query-layer concern
issues.suggestion: Extract BlackLabSnippetParser into a query/blacklab/ or utils/blacklab/ sub-package; move QueryResults DTOs to a model/ package; move PosGroup to a shared model/ or common/ package; consider a query/explore/ sub-package for SemanticFieldExplorer

issues.summary: PosGroup constants live in query/ but are imported by config/ and api/, which creates an unexpected upstream import direction
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.PosGroup
WordSketchApiServer imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.PosGroup
PosGroup contains only four String constants (NOUN, VERB, ADJ, ADV) with no query logic
Having config/ and api/ depend on query/ for a shared constant creates coupling that suggests the constant belongs in a lower-level shared package
issues.suggestion: Move PosGroup to a model/ or common/ package that sits below api/, config/, and query/ in the dependency graph

issues.summary: CQL/BCQL pattern manipulation logic is independently implemented in both api/PatternSubstitution and config/GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig, with no shared abstraction
evidence: PatternSubstitution.extractXposFromConstraint searches for 'xpos="...' and 'tag="...' in a constraint string
GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig.extractConstraintAttribute does the same via a regex, used by extractXposPattern/extractPosPattern
PatternSubstitution.escapeForRegex and GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig.escapeForRegex are identical one-liner methods duplicated across packages
Both packages parse CQL bracket tokens via manual index scanning with the same '[' / ']' logic
issues.suggestion: Consolidate CQL pattern utilities into a single grammar/ or query/cql/ utility class; both PatternSubstitution and GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig should delegate to it

issues.summary: A null GrammarConfigLoader is passed through from Main into WordSketchApiServer and then null-checked in every single endpoint handler
evidence: Main.handleServerCommand: GrammarConfigLoader grammarConfig = null; try { ... } catch (IOException e) { System.out.println("No grammar config found, using defaults."); } — null is silently accepted
WordSketchApiServer receives grammarConfig as a constructor parameter and stores it as a nullable field

Every handler that uses grammar config: 'if (grammarConfig != null)' — at least 6 distinct null guards in handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
Without grammar config, most endpoints either silently return empty results or return a 500 error — the behavior is inconsistent
issues.suggestion: Fail fast at startup if grammar config is missing (the server cannot serve meaningful results without it); alternatively use a non-null NullObject/default implementation rather than propagating null through the entire API layer

issues.summary: Both BlackLabQueryExecutor and GrammarConfigLoader perform file I/O in their constructors, mixing construction with resource acquisition
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor constructor: this.blackLabIndex = BlackLab.open(new File(indexPath)); — opens an index file during construction
GrammarConfigLoader constructor: String content = Files.readString(configPath); — reads and parses the config file during construction

GrammarConfigLoader constructor also throws IllegalArgumentException for business rule violations (e.g. missing 'version', presence of 'copulas') mixed with IOException for file access failures; callers must handle both exception types from a single constructor call
issues.suggestion: For BlackLabQueryExecutor, provide a static factory method or an open() method distinct from the constructor; for GrammarConfigLoader, separate file loading from validation so that IOException and IllegalArgumentException come from clearly distinct call sites

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer stores the com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer instance in a field set during start(), leaving the object partially initialized between construction and start()
evidence: private com.sun.net.httpserver.HttpServer server; — field is null until start() is called
stop() method: if (server != null) { server.stop(0); } — null guard confirms caller-visible partial-init state
start() has no guard against being called twice; a second call would create a new server object while the first continues running and the original reference is lost
issues.suggestion: Either create the HttpServer inside the constructor (before start()) so the object is fully initialized when returned, or use a builder pattern; add an idempotency guard in start() that throws IllegalStateException if called when already running

issues.summary: The grammar config path 'grammars/relations.json' is resolved relative to the JVM working directory at startup, creating a fragile environment dependency
evidence: var grammarPath = java.nio.file.Paths.get("grammars/relations.json"); — no ability to override from CLI
GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish() also hardcodes Path.of("grammars/relations.json")
The server command does accept --index for the corpus path but provides no equivalent --grammar option, forcing the grammar file to live at a specific CWD-relative location
issues.suggestion: Add a --grammar CLI option to handleServerCommand so operators can specify the config path; use the hardcoded path only as a default, and log the resolved absolute path at startup to aid debugging

issues.summary: handleRelationQuery and handleDependencyRelationQuery are structurally identical methods duplicated with only relationType string differing
evidence: handleRelationQuery (lines 341-377) and handleDependencyRelationQuery (lines 442-478) share identical bodies: config lookup, executeSurfacePattern call with same args, identical JSON collocations-building loop, identical response structure
The only meaningful difference is the relationType check ('SURFACE' vs 'DEP') and one extra 'relation' field label
Both methods build the same JSONArray collocations loop (lines 361-369 vs 462-469) verbatim
issues.suggestion: Extract a private buildCollocationResponse(exchange, lemma, relation, relationType) helper that both call with different relationType arguments, or collapse both into one method parameterised on relationType.

issues.summary: The /api/relations and /api/relations/dep endpoint lambdas duplicate the same loop body, differing only in the relationType filter
evidence: Lines 118-143 (/api/relations) and lines 146-173 (/api/relations/dep) iterate grammarConfig.getRelations() and build JSON objects with the same fields
The only difference between the two blocks is the filter string ('SURFACE' vs 'DEP')
issues.suggestion: Extract a private helper buildRelationsJson(String relationType) that both endpoints delegate to.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExplore mixes parameter parsing, exploration orchestration, and full JSON serialisation in one ~115-line method
evidence: Lines 484-526: parameter extraction and validation (seed, relationId, ints/doubles)
Lines 515-526: exploration execution via SemanticFieldExplorer
Lines 528-596: manual JSON response construction iterating seedAdjectives, discoveredNouns, coreAdjectives, and edges in four separate loops
issues.suggestion: Extract a toExplorationResponseMap(ExplorationResult result, ...) helper that converts the domain result into the response map, separating serialisation from the handler logic.

issues.summary: executeBcqlQuery in BlackLabQueryExecutor conflates three distinct phases in one 100-line method: hit collection, collocate frequency accumulation, and result ranking
evidence: Lines 228-277: single pass collecting HitRecord objects and building collocateFreqMap
Lines 280-308: second pass computing logDice and building ConcordanceResult list
The method also handles collocate extraction fallback logic inline, competing with BlackLabSnippetParser's responsibilities
issues.suggestion: Extract collectHits(...) and buildResults(List<HitRecord>, Map<String,Long>, long, int) private helpers, giving each phase a named boundary.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.getCorpusSize() always returns 0L, silently breaking any caller that expects a real corpus size
evidence: Lines 534-536: 'public long getCorpusSize() throws IOException { return 0L; }' — no comment, no exception, no delegation to blackLabIndex
The method name implies it returns the actual number of tokens or documents indexed
issues.suggestion: Either implement using blackLabIndex.mainAnnotatedField().tokenCount() or similar, or remove the method, or rename to getCorpusSizeUnimplemented() and add a TODO comment.

issues.summary: word1.replace("&","&") at line 851 is called before the null-check at line 856, causing NullPointerException when word1 is absent from query params
evidence: Line 847: 'String word1 = params.get("word1");' — returns null if key absent
Line 851: 'if (word1 != null) word1 = word1.replace("&", "&");' — actually this line IS guarded... wait re-reading: 'if (word1 != null) word1 = word1.replace(...)' — actually checking again
Line 851: 'if (word1 != null) word1 = word1.replace("&", "&");' — the null guard IS present here
However word2 at line 852 also has the same pattern: 'if (word2 != null) word2 = word2.replace("&", "&");' — also guarded

The null checks at lines 851-852 guard the replace calls correctly; the contract issue is the HTML-entity unescaping logic placed in a URL-query-param handler: '&' is an HTML entity, not a URL-encoded character, and should not appear in URL query strings
issues.suggestion: Remove the & replacement. URL-encoded ampersands are decoded by URLDecoder as '%26' → '&', not as '&'. If clients send malformed HTML-encoded URLs, validate and reject at the client layer.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti passes the raw pattern template to executeSurfacePattern for each seed, unlike the single-seed handler which calls getFullPattern(seed) first
evidence: Single-seed handler line 517: 'String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().getFullPattern(seed);' — substitutes lemma into pattern
Multi-seed handler line 650: 'String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().pattern();' — stores raw template once, reused for all seeds

Line 661: 'executor.executeSurfacePattern(seed, bcqlPattern, headPos, collocatePos, ...)' — the same unsubstituted template is passed for every seed, so the query runs against the generic pattern without lemma constraint
issues.suggestion: Inside the for-seed loop (line 659), compute the substituted pattern per seed: 'String seedPattern = relationConfig.get().getFullPattern(seed);' and pass seedPattern to executeSurfacePattern.

issues.summary: LogDiceCalculator.compute contains a dead condition: 'if (denominator <= 0)' can never be true given the earlier guard
evidence: Line 20: 'if (headwordFreq <= 0 || collocateTotal <= 0) { return 0.0; }' — both are guaranteed > 0 past this point
Line 25: 'double denominator = headwordFreq + collocateTotal;'
Line 27: 'if (denominator <= 0) { return 0.0; }' — since headwordFreq > 0 and collocateTotal > 0, their sum is always > 0; this branch is unreachable
issues.suggestion: Remove lines 27-29. If the intent is to guard against overflow producing a negative sum, add a comment explaining that; otherwise the check is misleading dead code.

issues.summary: SimpleTagger.determineVerbForm returns VBD (past tense) for 'am', but 'am' is VBP (first-person present indicative)
evidence: Line 355: 'if (word.equals("am")) return "VBD";' — VBD is past tense in Penn Treebank
'am' is the first-person present indicative of 'be', which Penn Treebank tags as VBP
The VERB_FORMS static map (line 64) also maps 'am' → 'VBP' (line 63: 'VERB_FORMS.put("am", "VBP")') — contradicting determineVerbForm

Wait, checking: line 62: 'VERB_FORMS.put("is", "VBZ");', line 63: 'VERB_FORMS.put("am", "VBP");' — the static map has the correct VBP, but determineVerbForm is called only from the AUX_VERBS path which does NOT check the static VERB_FORMS map
issues.suggestion: Change line 355 to 'if (word.equals("am")) return "VBP";' to match Penn Treebank convention and align with the VERB_FORMS static map.

issues.summary: The multi-seed loop uses a single shared pattern template rather than a per-seed substituted pattern, yielding different behaviour from the single-seed path
evidence: Line 650: 'String bcqlPattern = relationConfig.get().pattern();' — set once before the loop
Lines 659-675: for each seed, executeSurfacePattern is called with the same unsubstituted template
Single-seed path (line 517) calls getFullPattern(seed) to inject lemma="seed" into the pattern before querying
issues.suggestion: Move pattern resolution inside the seed loop and call getFullPattern(seed) for each seed to be consistent with single-seed logic.

issues.summary: RelationConfig record declares explorationEnabled as Boolean (boxed, nullable) but it is never treated as Optional — null is silently ignored at all usage sites
evidence: Line 311: 'Boolean explorationEnabled' in record declaration — boxed, allows null
Line 148: 'relObj.getBoolean("exploration_enabled")' — returns null when key is absent
toJson() at line 324: 'if (explorationEnabled != null) obj.put(...)' — null check present in serialisation but absent in any boolean evaluation context
No code path evaluates explorationEnabled.booleanValue() or uses it in a conditional after retrieval
issues.suggestion: Change to 'boolean explorationEnabled' primitive with a default: 'relObj.getBooleanValue("exploration_enabled", false)'. If null-ability is intentional (tri-state), document the semantic of null explicitly.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern overrides the interface default without @Override annotation
evidence: QueryExecutor.java lines 51-57: 'default List<WordSketchResult> executeSurfacePattern(...) throws IOException'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line 455: 'public List<QueryResults.WordSketchResult> executeSurfacePattern(...)' — no @Override annotation
If the interface signature changes, the compiler will not warn that this implementation no longer overrides anything
issues.suggestion: Add @Override to BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeSurfacePattern to make the override explicit and get compiler verification.

issues.summary: ExplorationResult.seedAdjectives, CoreAdjective, DiscoveredNoun.sharedAdjectives embed 'adjective' semantics but the explorer supports verb and noun relations equally
evidence: Lines 571-572: 'public final Map<String, Double> seedAdjectives' and 'public final List<CoreAdjective> coreAdjectives' — field names assume adjective collocates
Line 663: 'public static class CoreAdjective' — class name assumes adjective
Line 634: 'public final Map<String, Double> sharedAdjectives' inside DiscoveredNoun
exploreByPattern accepts any BCQL pattern and any relation name; collocates can be verbs (subject_of, object_of) or nouns, not just adjectives
WordSketchApiServer.handleSemanticFieldExplore line 543: iterates 'result.seedAdjectives' when the relation could be 'noun_verbs'
issues.suggestion: Rename seedAdjectives → seedCollocates, CoreAdjective → CoreCollocate, sharedAdjectives → sharedCollocates to reflect the generic collocate concept rather than a specific POS.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer contains both exploration (exploreByPattern) and comparison (compare, fetchExamples) functionality, but the name only communicates exploration
evidence: Lines 82-256: 'EXPLORATION MODE' section with exploreByPattern
Lines 258-383: 'COMPARISON MODE' section with compare(Set<String> seedNouns, ...)
Lines 393-408: fetchExamples method
Class Javadoc describes both modes, but the class name only suggests exploration
issues.suggestion: Rename to SemanticFieldAnalyzer or SemanticFieldService to encompass both modes, or split into SemanticFieldExplorer and SemanticFieldComparator.

issues.summary: getCorpusSize() name implies it returns actual corpus size but the implementation always returns 0
evidence: Lines 534-536: 'public long getCorpusSize() throws IOException { return 0L; }' with no comment
issues.suggestion: Either implement correctly, add a TODO/FIXME comment making clear this is a stub, or rename to make the stub status obvious.

issues.summary: getTotalFrequency swallows all exceptions and returns 0, silently corrupting logDice scores
evidence: getTotalFrequency (lines 316-330) wraps a broad catch(Exception e) that logs a warning and returns 0L
Callers in buildAndRankCollocates and executeBcqlQuery use the returned 0 directly in LogDiceCalculator.compute without any indication that frequency lookup failed
A missing or misconfigured 'lemma' annotation on the index would silently produce logDice=0 for every collocation rather than surfacing the configuration error
issues.suggestion: Propagate the exception or introduce a typed sentinel value (e.g. Optional<Long>) so callers can distinguish 'lemma not found' (0) from 'lookup failed'. At minimum, promote the log.warn to log.error and document the degraded-mode contract explicitly so callers know to skip logDice ranking when this returns 0.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer resources are not closed in try-with-resources, leaking on exception
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore (around line 515) creates SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor) and calls explorer.close() at line 526 after the explorer.exploreByPattern() call
The same pattern appears in handleSemanticField (line 747-749) and handleSemanticFieldExamples (line 825-827)
SemanticFieldExplorer implements AutoCloseable; if exploreByPattern or compare throws a RuntimeException the close() call is never reached and any owned executor is leaked
issues.suggestion: Replace the manual close pattern with try-with-resources: `try (SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor)) { ... }` in all three handler methods.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader constructor throws both IOException and IllegalArgumentException for what callers treat as a single initialization failure
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader constructor throws IOException for missing file and IllegalArgumentException for invalid content (e.g. missing version field, presence of deprecated 'copulas' key, empty relations array)

In Main.handleServerCommand the constructor is called inside a catch(IOException) block, meaning IllegalArgumentException (unchecked) will propagate past the catch and surface as an unhandled exception rather than a clean startup error message
Callers must know to catch two different exception types for what is logically one operation: loading config
issues.suggestion: Use a single checked exception type (IOException with a descriptive message, or a dedicated ConfigurationException) for all failure modes in the constructor. The validation failures are config errors not runtime bugs and should be catchable.

issues.summary: deleteRecursively silently swallows IOException with no log output
evidence: deleteRecursively (lines 221-227) catches IOException and ignores it entirely: catch (IOException ignored) {}
This is called from a shutdown hook registered during indexing to clean up temporary WPL chunk files
If the temp directory cannot be deleted (e.g. file lock, permission error) the failure is invisible; in contrast, similar silent swallows elsewhere at least emit log.warn
issues.suggestion: Add at minimum a log.warn call so failures are observable: catch (IOException e) { logger.warn("Failed to delete temp dir {}: {}", dir, e.getMessage()); }

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer inner DTO classes use public final fields while sibling QueryResults DTOs use private fields with getters
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.DiscoveredNoun, CoreAdjective, ExplorationResult, AdjectiveProfile and Edge all expose public final fields directly (e.g. public final String noun, public final double similarityScore)
QueryResults.WordSketchResult and ConcordanceResult in the same package use private final fields with getter methods (getLemma(), getLogDice(), etc.)
Both sets of classes are used as data transfer objects between the query layer and the API layer
issues.suggestion: Adopt one consistent style across all DTO types in the query package. The getter pattern in QueryResults is more standard Java and easier to serialize with JSON libraries; migrate SemanticFieldExplorer inner classes to private final fields + getters, or document the public-field style as intentional for immutable value types.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.getCorpusSize() returns hardcoded 0L and is not declared on the QueryExecutor interface
evidence: getCorpusSize() at line 534 returns 0L with no implementation body and no comment explaining why
The method is not on the QueryExecutor interface so it is not accessible polymorphically and cannot be tested through the interface contract
No caller in the codebase calls this method, making it dead code
issues.suggestion: Either remove the method if it is abandoned, implement it using BlackLab's corpus statistics API, or add it to the QueryExecutor interface with a Javadoc explaining its intended semantics.

issues.summary: handleConcordanceExamples applies manual '&' replacement that no other handler performs
evidence: Lines 850-852 in handleConcordanceExamples explicitly call word1.replace("&", "&") and word2.replace("&", "&") after HttpApiUtils.parseQueryParams has already URL-decoded the values
No other handler in the file applies this extra HTML entity decoding step
A comment at line 851 says 'browsers encode & as & in HTML' suggesting this was added as a workaround rather than a general fix
issues.suggestion: If browsers send HTML-encoded entities in query strings when following an href, fix the client-side URL construction instead of adding server-side workarounds. If server-side handling is needed, centralise it in parseQueryParams or a dedicated decoding helper and apply it uniformly.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer — the entire API layer with 10+ request handlers — has zero test coverage
evidence: No test file exists for WordSketchApiServer in src/test/
Handler methods include parameter validation, grammar config dispatch, error response logic, and result serialization — all untested
Bugs like unguarded Integer.parseInt on user params (500 instead of 400), inconsistent min_logdice defaults, and the & workaround are not caught by any test
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests for the handler methods using a mock QueryExecutor and a test HttpExchange (or an embedded HTTP server). At minimum: test parameter validation (missing/malformed params return 400), test the null grammarConfig fallback path, and test that valid requests return the expected JSON shape.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser — XML extraction utilities called on every query — has no unit tests
evidence: No test file exists for BlackLabSnippetParser

The class contains regex-based XML parsing (extractLemmaFromMatch, extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition, trimLeftXmlAtSentence, trimToSentence, detokenize) that is called in every query execution path in BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeBcqlQuery and executeSurfacePattern
Edge cases like null input, malformed XML, missing lemma attributes, and multi-word matches are handled by hand with fallback logic that is entirely untested
issues.suggestion: Add a BlackLabSnippetParserTest covering: null/empty inputs, valid XML with expected lemma extraction, position-based collocate extraction, sentence boundary trimming, and the detokenize punctuation rules. These are pure functions with no dependencies and are easy to unit test.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer core logic has no unit tests despite being the primary business value of the application
evidence: No test file exists for SemanticFieldExplorer
The class contains the intersection/ranking logic for semantic field discovery (exploreByPattern, compare) that is the core feature of the application
A mock QueryExecutor constructor exists (SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor)) specifically enabling testability without a real index, but no tests use it
issues.suggestion: Write unit tests using the mock-executor constructor. Test the exploration algorithm with a small deterministic mock that returns fixed collocation lists, verify intersection logic, similarity scoring, min_shared filtering, and the ExplorationResult/DiscoveredNoun structure. The testability hook already exists — it just needs tests.

issues.summary: PatternSubstitution has no tests despite performing regex-sensitive string mutations on BCQL patterns
evidence: No test file exists for PatternSubstitution

substituteCollocate performs positional token splitting and insertion into BCQL patterns — a fragile string operation where off-by-one errors or unbalanced brackets would silently produce malformed queries
escapeForRegex and extractXposFromConstraint are pure functions with no side effects, trivially unit-testable
issues.suggestion: Add PatternSubstitutionTest with cases: correct collocate substitution at position 1 and 2, preservation of xpos constraint, no-op on out-of-range position, null inputs, and regex-special characters in collocate names (e.g. collocate containing quotes or backslashes).

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern takes 10 parameters, far exceeding the readable API boundary
evidence: exploreByPattern signature: (String seed, int topPredicates, int nounsPerPredicate, int minShared, double minLogDice, String bcqlPattern, String simplePattern, String relationName, int headPos, int collocatePos)
The 6 parameters at the end (bcqlPattern, simplePattern, relationName, headPos, collocatePos) all derive from a single RelationConfig object that the caller already holds
The caller in WordSketchApiServer.handleSemanticFieldExplore already has relationConfig.get() and extracts each field individually before passing them in
issues.suggestion: Introduce an ExploreOptions or RelationContext parameter object grouping bcqlPattern, simplePattern, relationName, headPos, collocatePos. Alternatively, add an overload that accepts RelationConfig directly if the dependency is acceptable. This reduces the method to 5 or fewer parameters.

issues.summary: Sibling endpoints /explore and /explore-multi use different defaults for min_logdice (3.0 vs 2.0)
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore line 513: Double.parseDouble(params.getOrDefault("min_logdice", "3.0"))
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti line 648: Double.parseDouble(params.getOrDefault("min_logdice", "2.0"))
The two endpoints are documented as sibling exploration endpoints accepting the same min_logdice parameter; different defaults will produce inconsistent result sets when clients omit the parameter
issues.suggestion: Define a single constant (e.g. private static final double DEFAULT_MIN_LOGDICE = 3.0) and use it in both handlers. If the difference is intentional (multi-seed needs a lower threshold to find common collocates), document it in the endpoint Javadoc and surface it in the /api/relations response.

issues.summary: Integer.parseInt and Double.parseDouble are called on raw user query parameters without error handling, producing 500 responses for malformed input
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore lines 510-513: four consecutive parseInt/parseDouble calls on user params (top, nouns_per, min_shared, min_logdice) with no try-catch
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti lines 646-648: same pattern for top, min_shared, min_logdice
handleSemanticField line 744-745 and handleConcordanceExamples line 854: same pattern
A request like ?top=abc will throw NumberFormatException which propagates through the generic Exception handler as a 500 Internal Server Error instead of a 400 Bad Request
issues.suggestion: Wrap number parsing in a helper (e.g. HttpApiUtils.parseIntParam(params, "top", 15)) that catches NumberFormatException and either uses the default or sends a 400 response. This centralises the pattern and ensures consistent 400 vs 500 semantics for malformed input.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor interface defines three overlapping query methods with unclear boundaries between findCollocations, executeBcqlQuery, and executeSurfacePattern
evidence: findCollocations(headword, cqlPattern, minLogDice, maxResults) — original method
executeSurfacePattern(lemma, bcqlPattern, headPosition, collocatePosition, minLogDice, maxResults) — supersedes findCollocations for surface patterns
executeBcqlQuery(bcqlPattern, maxResults) — returns ConcordanceResult instead of WordSketchResult, different return type but similar semantic

The default implementation of executeSurfacePattern delegates to findCollocations (QueryExecutor.java line 56) meaning the two methods are interchangeable for the default case, obscuring which to call
issues.suggestion: Clarify the interface contract with Javadoc distinguishing the three methods. If findCollocations is superseded by executeSurfacePattern, mark it @Deprecated. Consider whether executeBcqlQuery belongs on a separate ConcordanceExecutor interface to separate collocate-ranking from concordance retrieval responsibilities.

issues.summary: No authentication or authorization is applied to any endpoint; wildcard CORS is universal
evidence: All 10+ endpoint handlers proceed directly to business logic with no token validation, API key check, or session verification
Every response sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * (HttpApiUtils lines 28, 44, 56, 67), permitting cross-origin requests from any domain
No request IP restriction, rate limiting, or audit logging exists anywhere in the server
issues.suggestion: If this server is intended for local use only, add a check that the remote address is loopback (127.0.0.1 / ::1) and reject other connections with 403. If broader deployment is planned, add token-based authentication before the application reaches production use. The pattern is at least consistent (no partial enforcement), but the wildcard CORS combined with no auth allows any page on the internet to make requests if the port is reachable.
